lints.name: auth_rls_initplan
lints.title: Auth RLS Initialization Plan
lints.description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
lints.detail: Table \`public.user_profiles\` has a row level security policy \`Users can update their own profile\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.

lints.description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
lints.detail: Table \`public.kb_search_history\` has a row level security policy \`Users can view their own search history\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.
lints.cache_key: auth_rls_init_plan_public_kb_search_history_Users can view their own search history

lints.description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
lints.detail: Table \`public.mdm_remote_action_audit\` has a row level security policy \`Users can view audit in their org\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.
lints.cache_key: auth_rls_init_plan_public_mdm_remote_action_audit_Users can view audit in their org

lints.description: Detects if calls to \`current_setting()\` and \`auth.<function>()\` in RLS policies are being unnecessarily re-evaluated for each row
lints.detail: Table \`public.sap_agents\` has a row level security policy \`sap_agents_update_org\` that re-evaluates current_setting() or auth.<function>() for each row. This produces suboptimal query performance at scale. Resolve the issue by replacing \`auth.<function>()\` with \`(select auth.<function>())\`. See [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security#call-functions-with-select) for more info.

lints.name: multiple_permissive_policies
lints.title: Multiple Permissive Policies
lints.description: Detects if multiple permissive row level security policies are present on a table for the same \`role\` and \`action\` (e.g. insert). Multiple permissive policies are suboptimal for performance as each policy must be executed for every relevant query.
lints.detail: Table \`public.applications\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`anon\` for action \`INSERT\`. Policies include \`{"Admins can manage applications in their organization","Users can create applications"}\`

lints.detail: Table \`public.settings\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`dashboard_user\` for action \`SELECT\`. Policies include \`{"Admins can manage system-wide settings","Users can manage their own settings","Users can view their own settings"}\`

lints.description: Detects if multiple permissive row level security policies are present on a table for the same \`role\` and \`action\` (e.g. insert). Multiple permissive policies are suboptimal for performance as each policy must be executed for every relevant query.
lints.detail: Table \`public.review_scores\` has multiple permissive policies for role \`supabase_privileged_role\` for action \`SELECT\`. Policies include \`{"Reviewers can manage scores","Users can view scores for their reviews"}\`

lints.name: auth_db_connections_absolute
lints.title: Auth DB Connection Strategy is not Percentage
lints.description: Using a percentage based allocation connection strategy for Auth can help to improve the server's performance when increasing instance size.
lints.detail: Your project's Auth server is configured to use at most 10 connections. Increasing the instance size without manually adjusting this number will not improve the performance of the Auth server. Switch to a percentage based connection allocation strategy instead.
